perturbation.original_text: 1.6 "Control" means, with respect to the relationship between or among two or more Persons, shall mean the possession, directly or indirectly, Source: RAND WORLDWIDE INC, 8-K/A, 4/2/2001 or as trustee or executor, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the affairs or management of a Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, as trustee or executor, by contract or otherwise, including, without limitation, the ownership, directly or indirectly, of securities having the power to elect a majority of the board of directors or similar body governing the affairs of such Person; provided that neither PlanetCAD nor shall be deemed to be controlled by any other Person or under common control with any Person that is not one of their respective subsidiaries.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 "Control" means, with respect to the relationship between or among two or more Persons, shall mean the possession, directly or indirectly, Source: RAND WORLDWIDE INC, 8-K/A, 4/2/2001 or as trustee or executor, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the affairs or management of a Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, as trustee or executor, by contract or otherwise, including, without limitation, the ownership, directly or indirectly, of securities having the power to elect a majority of the board of directors or similar body governing the affairs of such Person; provided that PlanetCAD shall be deemed to be controlled by Dassault Systemes if Dassault Systemes acquires more than 25% of PlanetCAD's voting shares, and neither PlanetCAD nor shall be deemed to be controlled by any other Person or under common control with any Person that is not one of their respective subsidiaries.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Control" excludes PlanetCAD from being controlled by any entity unless through its subsidiaries. By adding a provision that Dassault Systemes controlling more than 25% of PlanetCAD's voting shares constitutes control, we create an in-text contradiction. This contradicts Section 11.17 (a) which states "This Agreement may be assigned or otherwise transferred, by operation of law or otherwise without the express written consent of PlanetCAD and Dassault Systemes", as change of control can implicate if the contract needs to be assigned or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.17 Assignment; Subcontracting; Third Party Beneficiaries. (a) This Agreement may be assigned or otherwise transferred, by operation of law or otherwise without the express written consent of PlanetCAD and Dassault Systemes, but in such event the assigning Party shall give notice to the non-assigning Party and the non-assigning Party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement within the 30-day period following receipt of such notice.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excludes PlanetCAD from being controlled by any entity unless through its subsidiaries. The changed text introduces a new condition where Dassault Systemes acquiring more than 25% of PlanetCAD's voting shares constitutes control. This directly contradicts section 11.17(a) which states that 'This Agreement may be assigned or otherwise transferred... without the express written consent of PlanetCAD and Dassault Systemes'. The change in 'Control' could trigger assignment clauses, thereby necessitating consent, which is in direct conflict with the 'without express written consent' provision.

perturbation.original_text: 5.4 Share of Net Revenue. As financial consideration under this Agreement, Net Revenue will be shared by the parties as follows: (a) For so long as PlanetCAD hosts the Co-Branded Service, during which time PlanetCAD shall be the Billing Party, all Net Revenue derived from sales of the Co-Branded Service to Dassault Systemes Customers shall be apportioned [***] percent ([***]%) to Dassault Systems and [***] percent ([***]%) to PlanetCAD.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.4 Share of Net Revenue. As financial consideration under this Agreement, Net Revenue will be shared by the parties as follows: (a) For so long as PlanetCAD hosts the Co-Branded Service, during which time PlanetCAD shall be the Billing Party, all Net Revenue derived from sales of the Co-Branded Service to Dassault Systemes Customers shall be apportioned [***] percent ([***]%) to Dassault Systems and [***] percent ([***]%) to PlanetCAD. Dassault Systemes at its sole discretion, can alter these percentages at any time.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 5.4, it states how the revenue should be divided when PlanetCad is hosting. The text now includes "Dassault Systemes at its sole discretion, can alter these percentages at any time." This is contradictory because it provides a revenue apportionment rule, but it can unilaterally be modified at any time. This contradicts the intention to come to an agreement in good faith on how the revenue is being shared. The original text clearly divides the revenue. The modified version makes it unclear whether revenue division is fixed, because it can unilateral be determined by Dassault Systemes. This contradicts the need for mutual agreement regarding revenue sharing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4 Share of Net Revenue. As financial consideration under this Agreement, Net Revenue will be shared by the parties as follows: (a) For so long as PlanetCAD hosts the Co-Branded Service, during which time PlanetCAD shall be the Billing Party, all Net Revenue derived from sales of the Co-Branded Service to Dassault Systemes Customers shall be apportioned [***] percent ([***]%) to Dassault Systems and [***] percent ([***]%) to PlanetCAD.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows Dassault Systemes to unilaterally alter the revenue sharing percentages at any time, which directly contradicts the original agreement, which established a fixed apportionment. This change creates a strong in-text contradiction because it undermines the concept of a mutually agreed-upon financial consideration, as outlined in both the original and contradicted text. The ability to alter the revenue sharing at any time makes the original revenue apportionment unenforceable and introduces ambiguity regarding the financial obligations of each party.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1 Term. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall continue in force for three years from the Launch Date (the "Initial Term") unless earlier 12 13 terminated in accordance with this Section (the "Term"). The Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one year additional terms unless terminated by either party at least six months prior to the expiration of the then-current term.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 Term. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall continue in force for three years from the Launch Date (the "Initial Term") unless earlier 12 13 terminated in accordance with this Section (the "Term"). The Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one year additional terms, subject to Dassault Systemes's right to terminate at any time with 30 days' notice, overriding the six months' notice requirement for PlanetCAD.
perturbation.explanation: Section 10.1 originally states that the agreement automatically renews for one-year terms unless either party terminates at least six months prior to the expiration. The added clause states that Dassault Systemes can terminate at any time with only 30 days' notice, while PlanetCAD is still subject to the six-month requirement. This creates a contradiction, as the automatic renewal is no longer equally binding on both parties. This contradicts the mutual terms for contract ending.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1 Term. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall continue in force for three years from the Launch Date (the "Initial Term") unless earlier 12 13 terminated in accordance with this Section (the "Term"). The Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one year additional terms unless terminated by either party at least six months prior to the expiration of the then-current term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause allowing Dassault Systemes to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice, overriding the original six-month notice requirement for PlanetCAD. This creates a direct contradiction within Section 10.1, as the contradicted text specifies that 'either party' must provide six months' notice for termination, implying equal terms. The modified text introduces unequal terms for termination, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
